verdict.answer_first: B opens with a direct bottom line: “Yes, a few real problems”; A opens softer: “A few things” and delays the usable answer.
verdict.decisiveness: A is less bad: it clearly says “the real problem” and gives a concrete replacement; B ends with “If you tell me which,” which weakens the prescription.
verdict.fabrication: B fabricates a JS bug: “foo@bar\n passes” is false for this regex in JavaScript. A’s “~99% of typos” is unsupported, but less central.
verdict.actionability: A is easier to act on because it gives the safer replacement and the real validation rule: “no regex is sufficient… send a confirmation email.”
